# Dependencies — Line 1f: Taxable adoption benefits (Form 8839 line 31)

**Tax year:** 2025
**Form:** 1040 / 1040-SR / 1040-NR line 1f
**Source-of-truth mapping:** `Form1040.Line1f = Form8839.Line31` (the 2025 Form 8839 Part III line number; do NOT use the 2024 line 29 mapping).

Line 1f carries the TAXABLE residue of employer-provided adoption benefits after Form 8839 Part III applies the per-child exclusion limit, special-needs rule, MAGI phaseout, and foreign-finality timing. Line 1f may be NEGATIVE under IRC §137(a)(3) when prior-year benefits become retroactively excludable in the finality year or when the special-needs off-W-2 path produces an exclusion against zero current-year benefits.

---

## Outputs Produced

| Output | Return field | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `form1040.income.adoptionBenefits` | Form 1040 line 1f | `roundMoney(Form8839.line31)`; SIGNED (may be negative); persisted only when non-null |
| `form8839` | Full Form 8839 output model | Created when `hasBenefits || hasAdoptionInputs`; includes Part I + II + III + `section1372DisallowedFromW2BoxT` |
| `form8839.part3Line31TaxableBenefits` | Form 8839 line 31 | Mirrors line 1f; sign preserved |
| `form8839.part2Line13RefundableAdoptionCredit` | Form 8839 line 13 | OBBBA 2025 refundable portion; flows to Form 1040 line 30 (capped at $5,000) |
| `form8839.part2Line18NonrefundableAdoptionCredit` | Form 8839 line 18 | DEFERRED: will flow to Schedule 3 line 6c when Part II credit is fully wired |

Wired in `buildIncome()` at lines 7361-7362 (line 1z aggregation) and 7464-7466 (Income persistence).

---

## Constants (2025) — `ReferenceData.java`

| Constant | Value | Statutory basis |
|---|---|---|
| `ADOPTION_MAX_EXCLUSION_PER_CHILD` | $17,280 | IRC §137(b)(1) (CPI-indexed) |
| `ADOPTION_PHASEOUT_START` | $259,190 | IRC §137(b)(2)(A) (CPI-indexed) |
| `ADOPTION_PHASEOUT_RANGE` | $40,000 | IRC §137(b)(2)(B) (NOT indexed) |
| Phaseout end (derived) | $299,190 | `START + RANGE` |
| `ADOPTION_LINE11B_REFUNDABLE_CAP` | $5,000 | OBBBA 2025 (Part II line 11b) |

---

## Per-Child Computation (Form 8839 Part III)

```
For each child i:
  L19[i] = $17,280   (or resolved shared-adoption cap when applicable)
  L20[i] = priorYearEmployerBenefitsByChild["child{i+1}PriorYearBenefits"] ?? 0
  L21[i] = max(0, L19[i] − L20[i])
  L22[i] = currentYearBenefitsAllocation["child{i+1}Benefits2025"] ?? 0
  L24[i] = (hasSpecialNeeds && adoptionFinal) ? L21[i] : min(L21[i], L22[i])

Return-level:
  L23 = w2TotalBenefits                          (literal sum of per-child L22 since 2026-05-24 Gap #5 closure)
  L25 = MAGI
  L27 = (L25 <= $259,190) ? 0 : min(1.0000, (L25 − $259,190) / $40,000)
  L28[i] = roundMoney(L24[i] × L27)
  L29[i] = L24[i] − L28[i]
  L30 = Σ L29[i]
  L31 = L23 − L30                                (SIGNED; may be negative)

Form1040.Line1f = roundMoney(L31)
                + section1372DisallowedFromW2BoxT  (2026-05-23 closure; bypasses Part III)
```

---

## Input Statement Sources

| Statement | Field read | Use |
|---|---|---|
| `w-2` | `box12Entries[].code == "T"` + `box12Amount` | Summed by `sumW2AdoptionBenefits` → drives `w2TotalBenefits`; partitioned into Part III vs §1372 disallowance |
| `w-2` | `employeeSSN` | SSN attribution (taxpayer vs spouse) |

Box 12 code T totals INCLUDE pre-tax cafeteria-plan (§125) salary-reduction contributions for adoption expenses, not just direct employer payments. Per IRC §3121, code T amounts are excluded from box 1 but included in boxes 3 and 5.

No other statement type contributes to line 1f.

---

## Input Personal Forms

| Form ID | Firestore path | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `adoption-expenses` | `users/{uid}/adoption-expenses` | Conditional (when box T > 0 or special-needs path) | Children array, Part II credit fields, Part III benefit allocations, §1372 fields, shared-adoption fields |
| `filing-status` | `users/{uid}/filing-status` | Yes (for MFS gate) | `filingStatus` + `mfsOrHohLivedApartOrLegallySeparated` |

### Fields in `adoption-expenses`

#### `children` section

| Field | Purpose | Effect if absent |
|---|---|---|
| `moreThanThreeChildren` | Triggers multi-form attachment for > 6 children | Display only |
| `sharedAdoptionAnyChild` | Outer screening for IRC §137(b)(1) shared-adoption with non-spouse | If false/null: per-child overrides ignored; full $17,280 cap |
| `entries[].childFirstName` / `childLastName` / `childYearOfBirth` | Identity | Form validation only |
| `entries[].identifyingNumber` | Child SSN/ATIN/ITIN | Display only |
| `entries[].hasSpecialNeeds` | Triggers IRC §137(a)(3) off-W-2 exclusion | If false: standard `min(L21, L22)`; no off-W-2 path |
| `entries[].adoptionFinal2025OrEarlier` | Finalisation gate | Required for special-needs path; otherwise per-child blocking |
| `entries[].isForeignChild` | Foreign-finality gate | UI blocks save if foreign and not final |
| `entries[].bornBefore2008AndDisabled` | IRS Part I checkbox | No computation effect on Part III |
| `entries[].sharedAdoptionWithNonSpouse` | Per-child shared-adoption flag | Read only when `sharedAdoptionAnyChild == true` |
| `entries[].sharedAdoptionAllocatedShare` | Replaces $17,280 cap (clamped 0–$17,280) | Falls back to $17,280 if null with flag true |

#### `adoptionCreditPartII` section (Part II credit — partially deferred)

| Field | Purpose | Effect if absent |
|---|---|---|
| `priorYearAdoptionCreditByChild["child{N}PriorYearCredit"]` | Per-child credit carryforward | Stored on Form 8839 output |
| `qualifiedAdoptionExpensesByChild["child{N}QualifiedExpensesGross"]` | Per-child gross expenses | `net = 0`; no Part II base |
| `qualifiedAdoptionExpensesByChild["child{N}EmployerReimbursementToSubtract"]` | Pre-fills from Part III allocation; user can override | `net = gross`; §137(d) coordination still structurally enforced |
| `line15CreditCarryforward` | Return-level credit carryforward | Stored on Form 8839 output |

#### `employerBenefitsPartIII` section

| Field | Purpose | Effect if absent |
|---|---|---|
| `priorYearEmployerBenefitsByChild["child{N}PriorYearBenefits"]` | L20[i] per child | L20[i] = 0; full L19 exclusion available |
| `currentYearBenefitsAllocation["child{N}Benefits2025"]` | L22[i] per child | L22[i] = 0 for that child |
| `section1372AnyEmployer` | Outer screening for IRC §1372 over-2% S-corp disqualification | UI blocks save until answered when any W-2 box T > 0 |
| `section1372ByEmployer` | Map of W-2 entryId → boolean; true entries' box-T amounts bypass Part III and stack onto line 1f as taxable | Read only when `section1372AnyEmployer == true` |

#### `importedStatementFields` section (backend-only)

| Field | Purpose | Effect if absent |
|---|---|---|
| `imports.magiForAdoptionBenefitsExclusion` | MAGI for phaseout L25–L28 | Phase 1: phaseout fraction = 0. Phase 2 G1 fallback in `applyAdoptionCredit` auto-derives `autoMagi = AGI + Form 2555 lines 45/50 + Puerto Rico + Form 4563 line 15` and re-runs Part II + III; manual entry trumps G1 |

---

## Output to Forms

| Form | How it consumes line 1f / Form 8839 |
|---|---|
| Form 8839 (PDF) | Children 1–3 on main page; 4–6 on overflow page; `f8839_field_map_semantic.csv`; multi-page `buildPdfFieldValues(childStartIndex, includeTotals)` |
| Form 1040 line 1f | Receives `Form8839.line31` directly (signed) |
| Form 1040 line 30 | Receives `Form8839.line13` refundable adoption credit (Part II; capped at $5,000) |
| Schedule 3 line 6c (DEFERRED) | Will receive `Form8839.line18` nonrefundable adoption credit when Part II is fully wired |

Line 1f does NOT generate a separate Schedule entry; it is a single cell on Form 1040.

---

## Special-Needs Rule (IRC §137(a)(3))

When `child.hasSpecialNeeds == true` AND `child.adoptionFinal2025OrEarlier == true`:

- `L24[i] = L21[i]` (full remaining exclusion, regardless of L22[i])
- Allows exclusion of amounts NOT shown on W-2 box 12 code T, provided employer had a written qualified adoption assistance program
- Combined with `L23 = $0` (no W-2 box T) → line 31 = `$0 − L30` (NEGATIVE)
- The IRS-required "written qualified adoption-assistance program" condition is not separately verified; user's special-needs-final answer is trusted

---

## MFS Joint-Return Gate (IRC §137(f))

| Filing status | `mfsOrHohLivedApartOrLegallySeparated` | Exclusion result |
|---|---|---|
| Not MFS | n/a | Normal exclusion computation |
| MFS | `true` (lived apart 6 months / legally separated) | Normal exclusion computation |
| MFS | `false` / absent | Exclusion DISALLOWED; `line 1f = w2TotalBenefits` (full taxable); emit `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_MFS_EXCLUSION_DISALLOWED` (blocking) |

---

## IRC §1372 Over-2% S Corp Owner Partition (2026-05-23 closure)

When `section1372AnyEmployer == true`:

- `computeSection1372DisallowedFromEligibleW2BoxT` partitions W-2 box T amounts by `entryId`
- Entries flagged `true` in `section1372ByEmployer` bypass Part III entirely
- Their box T amounts stack directly onto line 1f as TAXABLE wages
- Non-blocking advisory `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_SECTION_1372_DISALLOWED` emitted
- Section 318 constructive-ownership attribution captured in question wording but not separately computed

---

## Validation Flags

| Flag | Trigger | Blocking |
|---|---|---|
| `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_FORM_REQUIRED` | W-2 box 12 code T > 0 AND no adoption-expenses form data | YES |
| `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_W2_REQUIRED` | Part III inputs present AND no W-2 code T (over-blocks special-needs off-W-2; Code Validation #11) | YES |
| `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_MFS_EXCLUSION_DISALLOWED` | `isMfs && !mfsLivedApartOrLegallySeparated` | YES |
| `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_CHILD_REQUIRED` | Benefits/adoption inputs present AND 0 children | YES |
| `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_FOREIGN_CHILD_NOT_FINAL` | Per child: `isForeignChild && !adoptionFinal` | YES |
| `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_FINALIZATION_REQUIRED` | Per child: `hasSpecialNeeds && adoptionFinal2025OrEarlier == null` | YES |
| `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_ALLOCATION_MISMATCH` | `Σ L22[i] ≠ w2TotalBenefits` (outside ±$1 tolerance) | YES |
| `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_OFF_W2_NOT_SPECIAL_NEEDS_CHILD_N` | Per child: no W-2 box T AND positive L20 or L22 AND NOT (special-needs && final) | YES |
| `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_TOO_MANY_CHILDREN` | `childEntries > 6` | NO |
| `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_SHARED_ADOPTION_CAP_SPLIT` | A child's L19 cap reduced via shared-adoption override | NO |
| `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_SECTION_1372_DISALLOWED` | Any §1372-flagged W-2 entry | NO |

---

## Compute Order

1. `loadPersonalForms()` provides `adoption-expenses`, `filing-status`
2. `loadStatements()` provides W-2 entries (box T sourcing)
3. `computeAdoptionBenefits(...)` at line 18464 — produces `line1f` + Form 8839 Part III lines 19–31 + Part II lines 2–13 (when MAGI available)
4. `buildIncome()` at line 7361 — `line1z = addNonNull(... line1f ...)` (signed)
5. `line9 = addNonNull(line1z, line2b, ...)` at line 7440
6. `finalizeForm8839PartII` at line ~22289 — second pass derives final MAGI from AGI + Form 2555 + Puerto Rico + Form 4563; re-runs Part II for refundable line 11b (G1 fallback)

`computeAdoptionBenefits` runs in `prepare()` BEFORE AGI is finalized. The MAGI for line 1f Part III phaseout currently reads `imports.magiForAdoptionBenefitsExclusion` once (Code Validation #9 — above-threshold filers may silently over-claim if `imports.magi` is absent).

---

## Key Invariants

- `Line 1f = Form 8839 line 31` (2025 mapping; NOT 2024 line 29)
- Line 1f CAN BE NEGATIVE per IRC §137(a)(3); no `max(0, ...)` floor at line 1f, 1z, 9, or AGI
- `max(0, ...)` floor applied only at line 15 (taxable income)
- `line 1z = addNonNull(1a, 1b, 1c, 1d, 1e, 1f, 1g, 1h)` — line 1f participates signed
- Form 8839 is RETURN-LEVEL (one per return), even on MFJ with both spouses receiving benefits
- §1372-disallowed box T amounts BYPASS Part III and stack onto line 1f as taxable
- Same expenses cannot be both excluded (Part III) and credited (Part II) — IRC §137(d); enforced structurally via gross-minus-reimbursement net for Part II line 5

---

## Out of Scope

- IRC §1372 attribution under §318 constructive-ownership rules (question captures the disqualification; constructive attribution not separately computed)
- Off-finality-year prior-year benefits for non-special-needs foreign children beyond `L20` rollup
- Multi-form 8839 attachment beyond 6 inline children
- Part II nonrefundable adoption credit → Schedule 3 line 6c wiring (deferred)
